title: Persistent GPU runtime / server mode + C ABI FFI + gpu-buffer handles
goal: Per-token decode can't pay stdlib.bc reload + CUDA ctx init each call. Provide ANY of: (a) persistent server mode that loads stdlib + holds the CUDA context once then accepts many eval requests (socket/stdin loop); (b) clean extern C ABI so a C++ MoE worker calls an Eshkol-compiled GPU GEMM/FFN as a linked fn; (c) gpu-buffer handles surviving across calls so resident expert weights aren't re-uploaded per token.
acceptance: a warm server answers >=1000 GEMM eval requests with no per-call stdlib reload
a gpu-buffer handle survives across N eval calls (weights uploaded once)
